dataset_generation_rule: A generation is born after this protocol and collector source are committed; its immutable activation hash names the open append-only generation. Later checkpoints bind the activation, terminal receipt hash, and sorted partition hashes without rewriting prior identities.
completed_candle_rule: Retain only a one-minute candle whose inclusive close_time_ms is strictly earlier than the provider server time captured for that cycle; never retain the still-open candle.
availability_evidence_policy: ak-historian.pr4b0-r1p4.availability-policy.v1; availability is born only in a durable committed-collector receipt and is never inferred from event timestamps.
clock_evidence_policy: Every cycle requires local timedatectl NTPSynchronized=yes evidence and hashes that evidence into every receipt; missing or unacceptable evidence fails closed.

retry_and_rate_limit_policy.provider_ban_behavior: bounded retry then fail and preserve the failed cycle record

canonical_serialization: UTF-8 JSON using Go encoding/json deterministic map-key ordering, UTC RFC3339Nano timestamps, one trailing LF, and SHA-256 with sha256: prefix; self-hash fields are omitted when calculating their identity.
raw_response_retention_policy: Retain each successful public kline response immutably under a manifest-relative content-bound cycle/symbol identity; temporary and cache files are excluded.
normalized_fragment_retention_policy: Retain each closed-schema normalized fragment immutably with normalization version, source authority, cycle, symbol, records, and canonical fragment hash.
partitioning_policy: UTC calendar day per symbol; expected sequence is exactly 1,440 one-minute opens for a closed day. Open days remain PARTITION_OPEN and missing candles are never fabricated.
hash_chain_policy: Each envelope binds the prior envelope hash and each nested accepted Historian receipt binds the prior accepted-receipt hash. Both chains start at the all-zero SHA-256 genesis and are append-only.
duplicate_and_conflict_handling: Byte/content-identical durable identities are idempotent; differing content at an existing immutable identity fails closed, increments conflict evidence, and blocks checkpoint eligibility.
restart_and_catch_up_behavior: Rebuild cursors from the verified durable receipt ledger, request from last open plus 60,000 ms, record the actual later availability time, never backdate, and expose discontinuities as physical/evidence gaps.
gap_semantics: A missing expected one-minute open is a physical gap; a record lacking a valid receipt/provider/clock chain is an evidence gap. Late catch-up can close a physical gap only at its actual receipt availability.
supervisor_mechanism: user-level systemd oneshot service plus persistent five-minute timer, with collector file lock and next-timer recovery
activation_sequence: validate accepted governance and Historian contracts
commit protocol, policies, collector, tests, and supervisor templates
build the exact committed collector with its source commit embedded
create immutable activation identity and zero-hash receipt genesis
install and start user-level durable supervisor
collect at least three scheduled full-universe cycles
verify and commit immutable activation evidence
security_boundaries: TLS verification uses the Go standard trust verifier
no credential or secret environment dependency
no exchange account, order, trader, testnet, or mainnet endpoint
network code is isolated to internal/prospective
candidate_evaluation_prohibition: Candidate execution, signal generation, result calculation, outcome inspection, Engine invocation, and real RIF lifecycle operations are prohibited.
